_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning ways to work well on our own and how to make good decisions when we need to be responsible for ourselves.

_source.w: We are learning ways to work well on our own and how to make good decisions when we need to be responsible for ourselves.
y: Being able to work independently helps us feel proud of what we can achieve by ourselves.
Making responsible decisions is a skill we use every day — from choosing how to spend our time to knowing when to ask for help.
Learning independence now prepares us for more responsibility as we grow older.
eg: We brainstorm strategies for staying focused when working on a task by ourselves, like removing distractions and breaking the task into smaller steps.
We discuss why asking for help is actually a smart strategy when working alone, not a sign of weakness.
We practise making a simple decision — like choosing which task to do first — and explain our reasoning.
We set up a work plan for an independent project, listing the steps we need to follow and ticking them off as we go.
We reflect on a task we completed independently and write about what went well and what we would do differently.
We role-play situations where we have to decide between doing what is easy and doing what is right, and talk about our choices.
